published_prior_art.cite: BorusyakJaravelSpiess2024: efficient imputation and precision comparisons; ChenSantAnnaXie2025: efficient EIF benchmark for modern DiD/ES estimators

repo_prior_art.cite: panel_honest_did_phase: cohort-vs-aggregate Honest-DiD sensitivity frontiers; q1_spectral_phase_transition: Markov event-window row-space visibility, neither duplicates BJS/SA/CS/W IF equivalence

published_prior_art.cite: ChenSantAnnaXie2025: closed-form EIFs and efficient estimators benchmarking TWFE, CS, SA, BJS, Wooldridge; no verified exact four-estimator quotient equality condition
SunAbraham2021, CallawaySantAnna2021, Wooldridge2021: homogeneous-effect/equivalent-estimand sanity corner

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: ChenSantAnnaXie2025 is underplayed: it derives closed-form EIFs and efficient variance bounds for modern DiD/ES parameters and explicitly benchmarks TWFE, CS, SA, BJS, Wooldridge-type estimators, so the proposal must separate its quotient-equality frontier from that stronger efficient-EIF benchmark.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: tier=field below novelty_target=flagship unless the redraft turns the Gamma condition into a frontier relative to the true ChenSantAnnaXie2025 EIF, or proves a genuinely new sharp equality threshold not implied by their efficient-bound geometry.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: Assumption 8 makes BJS the canonical gradient by assumption, while §4-§5 position the claim under the general parallel-trends staggered-DiD model where ChenSantAnnaXie2025 claims EIF-based estimators dominate existing BJS/SA/CS/W implementations; the model restriction needs to be pinned or the baseline changed.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The proposal still says non-BJS estimators share or differ in 'canonical gradients'; in semiparametric terminology the canonical gradient belongs to the parameter/model, so this should be stated as estimator influence functions versus the efficient EIF.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Rebase the frontier on ChenSantAnnaXie2025's EIF: define Gamma_m as the gap between each named estimator IF and the true efficient EIF, then state when BJS/SA/CS/W all attain the bound.
Add a concrete three-period, two-cohort worked matrix for Gamma_m and show which known estimator pair separates on it.
Narrow Assumption 8 to the exact BJS efficiency model if keeping BJS as baseline, and explicitly say why ChenSantAnnaXie2025's overidentification gains are outside that restricted model.

recommended_next_step: Revise the literature and statement around ChenSantAnnaXie2025 before Stage 0; the current kernel is plausible but not flagship-clean.
